_strict_reconciliation.basis: AU Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: may disclose to support providers in New Zealand and Singapore

for payroll verification we may link a date of birth 1988-04-31, home suburb East Narrami WA, internal role Warehouse Technician, company device tag AQL-AU-Tablet-07, masked payroll account Ref PAY-0031-XX, and superannuation member alias SUP-00-XY-789 to the employee profile
is restricted to purpose, date, and outcome categories and excludes diagnosis unless required by law

Retention is guided by our schedule: recruitment records 12 months, payroll and super records 7 years, trading policy acknowledgements 5 years, and system logs 90 days unless a security investigation extends them.

Personal information may include opinions about you and quasi-identifiers such as suburb, role, or device tag when reasonably necessary for our functions.
Intentional example of linked fields in a single workflow: for payroll verification we may link a date of birth 1988-04-31, home suburb East Narrami WA, internal role Warehouse Technician, company device tag AQL-AU-Tablet-07, masked payroll account Ref PAY-0031-XX, and superannuation member alias SUP-00-XY-789 to the employee profile.
Elsewhere, standalone descriptors such as “based in Melbourne”, “aged in your 30s”, or “contractor” appear in forms and are not used to identify you without additional checks

must_detect.matched_text: Retention is guided by our schedule: recruitment records 12 months, payroll and super records 7 years, trading policy acknowledgements 5 years, and system logs 90 days unless a security investigation extends them.

Ardent Quokka Holdings Limited (Australia) provides this Privacy Notice under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and the Australian Privacy Principles.
We collect employment, health and safety, payroll and limited credit-related information for HR, WHS, and procurement purposes, with ASX/ASIC compliance obligations referenced only at a policy level.
The term “insider list” and references to “MNPI” in our staff trading policy are generic and describe controls under the Corporations Act; this notice does not disclose any market-sensitive or non‑public deal data.
Personal information may include opinions about you and quasi-identifiers such as suburb, role, or device tag when reasonably necessary for our functions.
Intentional example of linked fields in a single workflow: for payroll verification we may link a date of birth 1988-04-31, home suburb East Narrami WA, internal role Warehouse Technician, company device tag AQL-AU-Tablet-07, masked payroll account Ref PAY-0031-XX, and superannuation member alias SUP-00-XY-789 to the employee profile.
Elsewhere, standalone descriptors such as “based in Melbourne”, “aged in your 30s”, or “contractor” appear in forms and are not used to identify you without additional checks.
We maintain records in Australia and may disclose to support providers in New Zealand and Singapore in accordance with APP 8 and s 16C; cross-border clauses require comparable safeguards and no MNPI sharing.
Health information collected for incident reporting is restricted to purpose, date, and outcome categories and excludes diagnosis unless required by law.
Transaction data for expense claims is stored with supplier name, date, and totals; invalid-looking numbers like 0000-000-000 are placeholders on sample forms only.
ASX announcements referenced in training materials are already publicly available and do not contain any personal data.
Retention is guided by our schedule: recruitment records 12 months, payroll and super records 7 years, trading policy acknowledgements 5 years, and system logs 90 days unless a security investigation extends them.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
